channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Soldier who blew up Tesla at Trump hotel left note saying blast was to be a 'wake up call' for US
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A highly decorated Army soldier who fatally shot himself in a Tesla Cybertruck just before it blew up outside the Trump hotel in Las Vegas left notes saying the New Year's Day explosion was a stunt to serve as a “wake up call” for the country’s ills, investigators said Friday.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, TARA COPP, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How filmmakers and actors in Asia and the Asian diaspora are expanding representation
item.description: It’s vastly different to work as an Asian actor in North American hubs such as Toronto, Los Angeles and New York — than in those in Asia, including Hong Kong and Taipei.
item.dc:creator: Terry Tang, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New Los Angeles County DA Nathan Hochman meets with Menendez brothers' family ahead of resentencing bid
item.description: Los Angeles District Attorney Nathan Hochman met with the family of Lyle and Erik Menendez, saying he had a "productive" meeting with the family.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Selena's killer, Yolanda Saldívar, files for parole, begins review process
item.description: Saldívar, now 64 years-old, was sentenced to life in prison on Oct. 26, 1995, with the possibility of parole after 30 years.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Mendez, WGN 9

item.title: Blinken heads to Asia, Europe on last expected trip as top US diplomat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken will embark on what is expected to be his final overseas trip in office this weekend, traveling to South Korea, Japan and France. The State Department announced Friday that Blinken would visit Seoul, Tokyo and Paris beginning Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: University of Michigan lab develops blood test to identify HPV-related cancers
item.description: Researchers at a University of Michigan lab have developed a “first-of-its-kind” blood test to detect head and neck cancers caused by human papillomavirus.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, NewsNation

item.title: Biden awards the Medal of Honor and Medal of Valor to military heroes and first responders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — On Feb. 15, 1951, Army Pvt. Bruno R. Orig was returning from a mission when he found his fellow soldiers under attack in what's now known as the Battle of Chipyong-ni.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rudy Giuliani insists he's not hiding assets at contempt hearing over $148 million judgment
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Rudy Giuliani seemed to favorably impress a judge with three hours of testimony Friday at a contempt hearing as he insisted he's not hiding assets from lawyers trying to recover a $148 million judgment for two Georgia election workers. Judge Lewis J.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Los Angeles County DA provides update on meeting with Menendez brothers’ family
item.description: (KTLA) - New Los Angeles County District Attorney Nathan Hochman held a press conference to provide information on Friday's meeting with about 25 family members of Erik and Lyle Menendez.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, Fox 5

item.title: Biden awards Medal of Honor to seven US Army soldiers
item.description: President Biden on Friday awarded the Medal of Honor to seven U.S. Army soldiers, most of whom received it posthumously for service during the Korea War, in his last ceremony as commander-in-chief awarding the country’s highest military honor.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, WGN 9

item.title: Golden Globes 2025: Who's nominated, hosting, presenting, the honorees, what they'll eat and more
item.description: BEVERLY HILLS, Calif. (AP) — The Golden Globes will return Sunday with major star appeal thanks to a slew of big name nominees, including Zendaya, Timothée Chalamet, Angelina Jolie, Daniel Craig, Denzel Washington, Ariana Grande and more.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden blocks $14 billion acquisition of US Steel by Japan's Nippon Steel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden has blocked the nearly $15 billion proposed deal for Nippon Steel of Japan to purchase Pittsburgh-based U.S. Steel — affirming his earlier vow on the presidential campaign trail to prevent the acquisition of Steeltown USA’s most storied steel company.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, JOSH BOAK and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Some USPS workers stealing mail: Inspector General report
item.description: Federal prosecutors in December charged two postal workers with stealing more than $1 million in business checks.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation

item.title: New Orleans barricade oversight in 'target area for terrorism' during prime season raises concerns
item.description: New Orleans locals and visitors have been questioning why a temporary barrier intended to prevent cars from entering Bourbon Street was set down instead of up on Jan. 1.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Video shows U.S. Border Patrol agent appear to knee K-9
item.description: FALFURRIAS, Texas (ValleyCentral) — The U.S. Border Patrol Union of the Rio Grande Valley has issued a statement on an incident of what appears to be an agent kneeing a Border Patrol K-9.
item.dc:creator: Veronica Salinas, KTLA 5

item.title: Finalized tax credit for cleaner hydrogen gets a cautious OK from some environmental groups
item.description: The Biden administration released long-awaited final rules Friday for a tax credit that will send billions of dollars to producers of cleaner hydrogen.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former 'Home Improvement' star Zachery Ty Bryan jailed
item.description: Former "Home Improvement" star Zachery Ty Bryan was arrested this week on a domestic violence charge in South Carolina, online jail records show.
item.dc:creator: Caleb McCusker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Orca that carried her dead calf for weeks in 2018 is doing it again
item.description: An endangered Pacific Northwest orca that made global headlines in 2018 for carrying her dead calf for over two weeks is doing so once again following the death of her new calf, in another sign of grief over lost offspring, researchers said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands of essential oils recalled due to risk of poisoning
item.description: About 3,750 units of Euqee Wintergreen Essential Oils have been recalled because the packaging is not child-resistant.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, NewsNation

item.title: New videos paint clearer picture of Trump Hotel Cybertruck explosion in Las Vegas
item.description: Two new videos have been released in relation to Wednesday's Cybertruck explosion outside the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas where a former Army soldier died.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nearly 700 Party City store leases, including San Diego area, heading to auction
item.description: Nearly 700 Party City store leases will be heading to auction next month, according to an announcement Friday.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, Fox 5

item.title: Live updates: Mike Johnson wins Speakership on first ballot after 2 'no' votes flip
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) succeeded in keeping his gavel for the 119th Congress, winning in the House's Speakership vote in one round Friday. Johnson, who had a one-vote margin of error, won 218-215 over Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.). Rep. Thomas Massie (R-Ky.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, NewsNation

item.title: GOP trust in election accuracy rises after Trump's victory: Survey
item.description: Trump has publicly questioned or aired inaccurate claims about the 2020 election results, leading many within his party to also contest the legitimacy of the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden honors leaders of Jan. 6 committee with civilian award
item.description: President Joe Biden awarded the Presidential Citizens Medal to 20 people, including leaders of the Jan. 6 congressional committee on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WGN 9

item.title: Surgeon general calls for cancer warnings on alcohol
item.description: Alcoholic beverages should carry a prominent warning label about the link between alcohol and cancer, according to the U.S. surgeon general.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: Here’s what Congress’s first day will look like
item.description: The 119th Congress kicks off Friday as lawmakers return to Washington with a long January to-do list and a packed first day. Confirmation hearings for President-elect Trump’s Cabinet nominees are set to get rolling in the coming weeks, and Trump himself will be inaugurated in less than three weeks.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Federal appeals court strikes down Biden net neutrality rules
item.description: A federal appeals court ruled this week the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) lacked the authority to restore certain net neutrality rules last year, handing a blow to FCC Democrats and Biden administration officials who pushed for revived open internet measures.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jill Biden got most expensive gift from a foreign leader in 2023. Here's what it was
item.description: President Joe Biden and his family were given tens of thousands of dollars in gifts from foreign leaders in 2023, according to an annual accounting published by the State Department on Thursday, with first lady Jill Biden receiving the single most expensive present: a $20,000 diamond from India’s le
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: An orca that carried her dead calf for weeks in 2018 is doing so once again
item.description: An endangered Pacific Northwest orca that made global headlines in 2018 for carrying her dead calf for over two weeks is doing so once again following the death of her new calf, in another sign of grief over lost offspring, researchers said.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New Orleans revelers narrowly escape path of speeding truck in 'tactic of choice among terrorists': expert
item.description: A video from Bourbon Street in New Orleans shows Shamsud-Din Jabbar driving a pickup truck toward a crowd of revelers, who narrowly escape the path of the fast-moving vehicle on New Year's Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 dead and 19 injured in Southern California plane crash
item.description: FULLERTON, Calif. (AP) — Two people died and 19 were injured Thursday when a small plane crashed through the roof of a sprawling furniture manufacturing building in Southern California where at least 200 people were working, police said.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and DAMIAN DOVARGANES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump blames ‘open borders’ for New Orleans, Las Vegas attacks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump blamed the situation at the U.S.-Mexico border for the recent attacks in New Orleans and Las Vegas as both cases are being investigated.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, Fox 5
